data.title: Yu zhong scaling and items, rotation

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Perfect CC Chain: When Tigreal uses his Ultimate (Implosion) to pull and stun multiple enemies, it creates the perfect opening for Yu Zhong to dive in and land his full skill combo without missing.

counters.counter_desc: Difficulty in Laning: In the EXP Lane, Yu Zhong must be very careful with his skill placement. If he uses his dash skills recklessly, Phoveus will constantly stick to him, making it hard for Yu Zhong to escape or win a trade.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Strategy: Focus on poking the enemy with your 1st skill (Dragon Tail) while they try to last-hit minions.

Pro Tip: Try to trigger your passive (Cursing Touch) as much as possible. Once the Sha Essence erupts, you will gain a massive HP lead, allowing you to force the enemy out of the lane or even secure an early kill.

Precaution: Be careful of early ganks from the enemy Jungler before you reach level 4. Keep an eye on the map.

Strategy: Once you have your Ultimate, start rotating to the mid lane or turtle fights. Your primary goal is to use Black Dragon Form to zone out the enemy Jungler during objective contests.

Combo: Use your Ultimate to fly behind the enemy, transform back into Dragonoid form, and use Petrify to lock down the core carries (Mage/Marksman).

Objective: Push your lane first before roaming to ensure your turret stays safe.

Strategy: In the late game, you are the team's primary initiator. Do not show yourself on the map too early; wait for the enemy to gather, then dive from the flank using your Ultimate.

Targeting: Always prioritize the enemy's backline. Even if you don't get the kill, forcing the Marksman to retreat wins the team fight for your side.

Sustainability: Trust your Queen's Wings and Oracle combo. Even at low HP, your passive can turn the tide of a fight instantly.

emblems.emblem_desc: This setup prioritizes Adaptive Attack to win the lane early, while the combination of Spell Vamp and HP restoration ensures that the "Black Dragon" remains an unkillable force in team fights.

equips.equip_desc: With this build, you have enough damage to be a threat while remaining incredibly difficult to kill thanks to the synergy between Oracle, War Axe, and your passive.

hero.hero_overview: Yu Zhong, the Black Dragon, is a dominant fighter who excels in diving deep into the enemy backline. As a powerful Initiator, he forces enemies to scatter or face the wrath of his dragon form. His unique mechanics revolve around his passive, Cursing Touch, which allows him to sustain himself in the middle of chaotic team fights while dealing massive Burst damage. He is typically played in the EXP Lane, where he can bully most opponents and rotate quickly to help the team.
hero.hero_strength:
High Sustainability: His passive allows him to recover HP while dealing damage, making him incredibly hard to kill in 1v1 or team fights.
Backline Menace: With his Ultimate (Black Dragon Form), he can easily bypass tanks and target the enemy's Marksman or Mage.
Crowd Control & Mobility: He possesses multiple CC skills to disrupt enemy formations and a reliable dash to chase down fleeing targets.
Strong Early to Mid Game: Yu Zhong can dominate his lane early on, creating a huge advantage for the team before the late game starts.
hero.hero_weakness:
Weak to Hard CC: While in human form, he is vulnerable to "Suppress" or heavy crowd control which can prevent him from triggering his passive.
High Skill Cap: Managing his Sha Essence and timing his transformations requires good mechanical skill and game sense.
Reliant on Ultimate: Without his Black Dragon Form, he loses a significant amount of his initiation power and safety.

data.recommend: Master the power of the Black Dragon with this comprehensive guide! This build and strategy focus on maximizing your Burst Damage and Initation potential, ensuring you can dominate the EXP Lane

spell.spell_desc: Petrify is the ideal Battle Spell for Yu Zhong to maximize his combo potential and crowd control. Since Yu Zhong needs to stay close to enemies to trigger his passive, Cursing Touch, Petrify helps lock them in place, ensuring his skills land accurately.

data.title: "Backline menance: How to Dive and Delete Carries With Yu Zhong"

cooperates.cooperate_desc:
Yu Zhong (YZ) and Thamuz are both strong solo lane fighters in Mobile Legends, but they generally act as counter-picks to each other rather than a "perfect match". Their pairing is often a difficult matchup in the EXP lane, with the winner largely depending on player skill, timing, and item builds. 
Laning Matchup Dynamics
Early Game (Levels 1-3): Thamuz usually has the advantage in the very early game due to his wider poking range and strong damage with his passive and ultimate, especially if he uses the Vengeance battle spell. Yu Zhong often has to play safe, clear waves, and avoid an all-out fight until he has more stacks of his passive or his ultimate is ready.
Mid-to-Late Game: The matchup becomes more balanced and skill-dependent. Yu Zhong excels in team fights with his crowd control, zoning capabilities, and ability to dive into backlines. Thamuz, on the other hand, is a strong 1v1 duelist who thrives in extended fights, using his high sustain and attack speed to outlast opponents.
Key Items: Anti-heal items like Dominance Ice and Sea Halberd are crucial in this matchup, as both heroes rely heavily on their regeneration and spell vamp to survive. 
Team Composition
While they are rival laners, they can both be viable picks in a team composition as they serve slightly different roles. 
Thamuz is better for split-pushing and locking down specific targets.
Yu Zhong provides more utility in large-scale team battles with his ultimate, which can control multiple enemies at once. 
Ultimately, the choice between them or how they fare against each other in a game depends on the specific match's flow, the other heroes in the game, and player execution. There is no inherent "perfect match" synergy between the two heroes themselves; it's a matter of who can best counter the other's strengths at different stages of the game.

counters.counter_desc:

Their pairing is often a difficult matchup in the EXP lane, with the winner largely depending on player skill, timing, and item builds.

hero.hero_overview:
Yu Zhong is a powerful, highly sustainable Fighter hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang (MLBB), known as the "Black Dragon". He is a dominant force in the EXP Lane, capable of dealing massive area-of-effect (AoE) damage and regenerating HP, making him difficult to defeat in extended fights. 
Gameplay Overview
Yu Zhong's playstyle revolves around applying his passive, "Sha Residue," which, upon reaching 5 stacks, deals damage, restores a percentage of his lost HP, and grants a burst of movement speed. This mechanic allows him to thrive in chaotic team fights and sustain himself in the lane without frequent returns to base. 
Key Mechanics:
Sustain & Durability: His self-healing and HP regeneration make him an excellent brawler who gets stronger the longer a fight lasts.
High Mobility & Initiation: His ultimate ability, Black Dragon Form, allows him to transform into a large black dragon, ignore obstacles, gain crowd control immunity, and fly across the map to engage or escape team fights.
Versatility: He can fill multiple roles within a team composition, acting as a frontline, a diver to reach enemy backlines (like marksmen and mages), or an initiator.
Skill-Dependent Combos: Mastering his skill combinations is crucial for maximizing his damage output and applying the necessary stacks of his passive.
hero.hero_strength:
Yu Zhong is a powerful, versatile Fighter hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang known for his strong sustain, high area-of-effect damage, and excellent mobility. His strength lies in his ability to thrive in extended team fights and dive the enemy backline effectively with his ultimate. 
Core Strengths
Exceptional Sustain: His passive, Cursing Touch, allows him to heal a percentage of his lost HP when he lands skills, making him very difficult to take down in extended fights.
High Versatility: Yu Zhong can fill multiple roles within a team composition (frontline, diver, initiator, ganker) and is strong at virtually all stages of the game, from early lane dominance to late-game team fights.
Strong Mobility and Initiation: His ultimate, Black Dragon Form, gives him control immunity, allows him to travel over obstacles, and increases his max HP, providing excellent map rotation and the ability to initiate fights and escape safely.
Area of Effect (AoE) Damage: All of his skills are AoE, making him a powerhouse in team fights where he can hit multiple enemies and quickly stack his passive. 
Optimal Battle Spells
The choice of battle spell is flexible and depends on the enemy team composition and your playstyle: 
Vengeance: Excellent for sustainability in long team fights, providing damage reduction and reflecting damage. This is a top choice if you plan to dive into the middle of the enemy team.
Petrify: A great option for setting up his skill combos (especially after his third skill or ultimate) by locking enemies in place, allowing for easier passive stacking and burst damage.
Flicker: Useful for repositioning, escaping a bad situation, or catching misplaced enemies.
Execute: Can help secure kills against tankier heroes or those attempting to escape. 
Build and Playstyle
Yu Zhong's strength comes from his sustain and damage output, not pure defense items. A typical build focuses on a mix of offense and defense: 
Starting Items: Focus on damage items like War Axe and Hunter
hero.hero_weakness:
To counter Yu Zhong in Mobile Legends, focus on heroes with high mobility, CC, or sustain like Chou, Wanwan, Uranus, Esmeralda, or Kaja, use anti-heal items such as Sea Halberd or Dominance Ice, and employ strategies like kiting, freezing the lane, and interrupting his Dragonoid form to prevent him from stacking his passive and healing excessively.  
Hero Counters
Chou: High mobility, CC (ultimate interrupts his healing/form). 
Wanwan: Can dodge his skills and kite him effectively in her ultimate. 
Uranus/Esmeralda: High sustain heroes that can out-trade him in lane if played safely. 
Kaja: His ultimate (Divine Judgment) suppresses Yu Zhong, stopping him even in Dragonoid form. 
Gloo: Can stick to him and disrupt his form. 
Thamuz/X.Borg: Good for trading and dealing with his sustain in the EXP lane.

hero.hero_overview:
Yu Zhong is probably the best exp lane hero right now, he has got a balanced amount of CC , sustain, and damage. Moreover his ultimate, is crucial in teamfights. Don't play selfish on Yz , afterall exp is a second tank.

Don't be afraid to dive in to make sets for your team.
hero.hero_strength:
EARLY TO MID GAME : Yz has a pretty decent lane clear, you just have to be careful of enemy ganks till level 4. After that you can start playing aggressive. Try to zone their carries during lord and turtle.Marking their jungle during objectives will help your team secure them easily .

LATE GAME : Yz in late is easy to burst and kill, don't get isolated from your team, play like a tank, zone enemy team during objectives but don't commit alone. Don't be shy to use your ultimate just to zone enemies away.

 For example, if your team is trying to force a turret , you can ult and zone them preventing the enemies from defending.
hero.hero_weakness:
Keep an eye on the map :  during the early game, don't overextend if their mid is missing. If you get ganked early, you won't get lvl 4 before turtle, resulting in your team at a huge disadvantage.

Know your limits : Know which hero counters you and build accordingly. Adjust your play style according to the enemy heroes

data.recommend: Seasoned Exp laner and You Zhong main , sharing my experience with you guys.

spell.spell_desc: Petrify is the most viable option on yz, as when it is combined with his S3 , it has huge crowd control effects which can turn the tide of team fights in your favour.

dominants.dominant_desc: Yu Zhong is really powerful in early to mid-game. You can pick off enemy during the laning phase. Upgrade your first skill when the match starts. Try to kill the minions first to get to level two. Then, you can go ahead and upgrade your second skill. To activate his passive in early game, use the end of his first skill to maximise its damage and to get 2 stacks. After that you can use his second skill on the enemy to activate his passive.

emblems.emblem_desc: I usually use Killing Spree as his main emblem because he can easily outplay enemy heroes when he's getting ambushed or in a 1v1 situation, he also gains movement speed which is great to escape from enemy

equips.equip_desc:
The key to this equipment is the War Axe. He can easily kill enemies when the War Axe's stack is at max. You can continuously hit jungle or minions without killing it to keep the War Axe's stack. 

Queen's Wings is also useful because it can reduce the cooldown of your skills and also make you last longer in a war. 

Hunter strike is used in this build to increase his damage and movement speed to chase squishy enemies.

Oracle is mostly used in Yu Zhong's build to maximise his healing ability because his passive is really important in team fights to last longer 

Dominance Ice is used to counter enemies with high healing ability such as Estes,Floryn,Angela,Etc... (You can switch between Dominance Ice and Immortal if the enemy doesn't have high healing heroes)

spell.spell_desc: Petrify is really useful when used on Yu Zhong, especially to pick off enemy and war. You can combo petrify with Yu Zhong's 3rd skill to maximise his control ability

i mostly use this build against other sustain heroes. if the hero against me cant sustain in fights, i will switch dominance ice with brute force to increase my damage and hp a bit.

counters.counter_desc: Full build Layla can take out Yz pretty fast, even in his flying dragon form. But can still be beaten if you're quirky
Can be a strong counter for Yu Zhong if pokes and maintains distance properly
She has strong hit and run ability. A good Benedetta can outmaneuver a Yu Zhong
They are pretty even except ruby can out sustain Yu zhong. Not absolutely impossible for Yu Zhong to beat ruby though

People think Dyrroth is an absolute counter to Yu Zhong and that's really not the case. Its quite easy to defeat a Dyrroth as long as the user and circumstance are in favor. Though a good Dyrroth user may be able to out maneuver a Yu Zhong

dominants.dominant_desc: Avoid death but play aggressively during early to Mid stages of Yu Zhong. Make sure to use his passive to the fullest and connect his skills in a fast manner. You can both be a fast hand or a slow hand Yu Zhong. People just don't know this enough. During late stage of the game you'll absolutely need to initiate with your team and focus on taking+dishing out damage to the enemy. Do not fight alone. Don't lose confidence but rely on team as well. Take out their fighters or squishy heroes by yourself only when you're confident you'll win and not die.
dominants.dominant_title: Early to exp and conservative late

emblems.emblem_desc: This one is better for team fights when Yu Zhong is up against multiple enemies

equips.equip_desc: Yu Zhong's core items are composed of War axe and queens wings for the most part, alongside dominace ice. Though the last three slot mostly depends on the enemy line up so adjust accordingly. If you have good def and doing well and require dmg just get brute force. If it's safe for you to get more regen add oracle. If you need better physical defense add antique cuirass.

hero.hero_overview: Meet Yu Zhong, The reincarnation of the black dragon who's ultimate goal is to unify the entire cardia riverlands through conquest. Yu Zhong is a strong fighter in the exp lane.
hero.hero_strength: Yu Zhong can actually dominate most fighters if used well. His lane clearing speed is decent and most importantly he excels in team fights and shines most against multiple opponents. He can reduce half of the entire teams health all by himself and even kill some if used right, making it easier for the team to sweep in and wipe out. He is quite tanky so it's best you build him with the usual fighter build and add tanky items accordingly. Yu Zhong's max potential can be only brought out when he or his team coordinate with each other
hero.hero_weakness: Yu Zhong may lack sustain when faced against anti regens + burst enemies. Its necessary you always initiate when you have your team around. One of his core weakness is that he can be taken out easily by a long distance burst marksmen. Yu Zhong is heavily team reliant.

data.recommend: Read this and like my detailed guide If you're striving to be a good fighter/Yu Zhong user

spell.spell_desc: Two spells can be used for yz. One is vengeance other is Petrify. Vengeance is good for making yz tank and last more during fights. While Petrify allows him to stop and land his skills completely on the enemy, Pair Petrify with his third skill for max potential

cooperates.cooperate_desc: This only depends on you, but Angela's ult could enable Yu Zhong to reach it's full potential. By providing speed boost and heal, you will be unstoppable.

counters.counter_desc: Arlott could easily dash out and dodge Yu Zhong's 1st skill, this could be an advantage unless you know how to time.

dominants.dominant_desc:
For his gameplay, always strike the opponent with the outer layer of your 1st skills, this will deal much more damage. You can cut lanes if you want, don't be afraid since Yu Zhong has a strong passive which will regenerate his hp. 
For his combo: 3rd + petrify, the petrify will prevent the enemy from escaping your 3rd skill which will airborne the enemy and this could give you time to + 1st + 2nd this will trigger your passive and it'll continuously damage the opponent.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Thrill: For damage dealt.
Festival of Blood: For spell vamp and sustainability.
Quantum Charge: For mobility.

equips.equip_desc: Since you don't need that much of damage for Yu Zhong, i recommend this sustainable item for him, this will allow him to stay longer in team fight and dominate the game.

hero.hero_overview: Yu Zhong is a hybrid that can blend in any type of exp gameplay as long as you maximize its ability. Yu Zhong has 4 skills which gives him an advantage to rule the exp lane, apart from being a fighter, he can also deal a good amount of damage using his passive. For his gameplay, first you need to know your opponent, if the opponent depends on dash, use 2nd skill and strike it on minions and use your basic attack on your exp opponent and hit it one more time. This will enable Yu Zhong's passive which can take a continues damage to the enemy and also heals itself.
hero.hero_strength: As for Yu Zhong's strength, Yu Zhong relies on his sustainability and passive. This'll allow him to control, guard, and charge the enemies without thinking of him being burst out quickly. Yu Zhong has a strong regeneration ability, which also allows him to keep up in team fights and can even withstand 1v5 scenarios.
hero.hero_weakness: Like other heroes in MLBB, Yu Zhong definitely have it's weaknesses. But this will only depend on your gameplay and play-style. These weaknesses are: heroes like wanwan, arlott, cici, irithel and other heroes with CC effect that could prevent Yu Zhong from sustaining.

spell.spell_desc: Petrify, if you're an EXP laner Yu Zhong, i recommend using petrify for late game crowd control and AOE. Using petrify along with his 3rd skill could be a team setter for late game.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Most roam heroes with CC are a perfect pair for Yu Zhong

dominants.dominant_desc: Just play safe in early  especially when your enemies have high mobility heroes or have Esmeralda. Don't go too deep into the enemies base especially when you don't have items. In Late game just target the enemies main damage dealer like their Gold Laner especially Mid Laners as Most Mages like Valir can reduce your regen and deal high damage to you.

emblems.emblem_desc: For Sustain Yu Zhong, You can also replace War Cry with Brave Smite for more regen.

equips.equip_desc: This build will maximize Yu Zhong's regen and lifesteal,because of War Axe,Queen's wings and Oracle. Dominance Ice will help to counter high regen heroes and Brute Force will counter CC and slow heroes.

hero.hero_overview: Strong Sustain exp with strong lifesteal.
hero.hero_strength: Strong in early game to late game because of lifesteal. Can counter most heroes with regen In early game like Argus,Uranus,Terizla,etc. Can also defeat them in late game if played right. Can disengage to fights easily with his ultimate.
hero.hero_weakness: True damage heroes like Alpha and Lesley. Can also be countered by high mobility heroes especially when they have sea halberd or Dominance ice as their item.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Angela is a perfect support duo for Yu Zhong, offering mobility, protection, and continuous crowd control. With her ultimate Heartguard, Angela can instantly attach to Yu Zhong as he dives in with Black Dragon Form, giving him a powerful shield and healing throughout the fight.

counters.counter_desc: As soon as he casts Black Dragon Form, activate the Tortoises Puissance ultimate and follow him. Use Baxia-Shield Unity and Shield of Spirit to chase and slow him down when he tries to escape or pursue your allies.

dominants.dominant_desc: In the laning phase, you should prioritize using Soul Grip on the enemy minions to clear them efficiently and then use basic attacks on the enemy hero to apply Sha Residue stacks, which depend on how many minions you have hit with the skill. Once you reach level four, consider using your Black Dragon Form to either assist in team fights near the EXP lane or help the jungler secure the Turtle. In team fights, a typical combo involves activating Black Dragon Form, engaging the enemys backline, deactivating your ultimate, and then using Petrify, Dragon Tail, Soul Grip, and Basic Attacks. If the enemy attempts to escape by using Flicker or another mobility skill, you can respond by using Furious Drive to close the gap and secure the kill.

emblems.emblem_desc: Thrill will help you bully the enemy laner and dominate the lane, so take that as your tier-one talent. Festival of Blood on the other end, allows you to stay in fights longer thanks to its Spell Vamp. Top it off with War Cry to unleash the full might of the hero.

equips.equip_desc: Start with War Axe for increased damage and mobility. Next, get Oracle to boost his shield and healing. It also strengthens the effect of Cursing Touch, providing decent magic defense. In the mid-game, buy Queens Wings for damage reduction and lifesteal to survive team fights. Then, get Hunter Strike for extra speed and damage to chase down enemies. Finally, finish with Bruteforce Breastplate for added defense and mobility, which keeps Yu Zhong strong throughout the game.

hero.hero_overview: Yu Zhong, the Black Dragon, is a fighter hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang with three forms and a secondary resource that is powered by his passive.
hero.hero_strength: His ultimate lets him transform into a large black dragon, giving him crowd control immunity and the ability to knock back enemies.
hero.hero_weakness: He is a complex hero, and it could take a while to fully understand his skills, and once you do, you will still need to properly manage his Sha Residue to succeed.

spell.spell_desc: Petrify is an excellent battle spell for Yu Zhong because it perfectly complements his aggressive, crowd-control-heavy playstyle. By using Petrify, you can instantly immobilize enemies, allowing you to land your skills more effectively and secure kills.
